title: AP issues warnings as record temperatures and heatwaves hit state - The Hans India
full_text:
AP issues warnings as record temperatures and heatwaves hit state
The Andhra Pradesh Meteorological Department has issued important warnings, predicting record-breaking temperatures across several districts on Saturday. Heatwaves are expected in 64 mandals, including Vizianagaram, Manyam, Palnadu, and Prakasam. Lightning and rain are also reported in various regions, prompting advisories for public precautions against the intense heat.
Prakhar Jain, MD of the Disaster Management Authority, reported a maximum temperature of 45.9°C in Piduguralla, Palnadu. He revealed that 252 mandals across 20 districts experienced temperatures above 41°C, significantly impacting the state. A surface trough extending from South Odisha is causing sporadic rains with lightning in districts such as Srikakulam, Vizianagaram, and Tirupati.
The weather is expected to slightly cool on Sunday and Monday, with some districts experiencing cloudy weather and gusty winds. Farmers and outdoor workers are advised to exercise caution, avoid sheltering under trees during thunderstorms, and carry umbrellas and breathable clothing when outdoors.
